name: Lagos de Saliencia — circular in the Parque Natural de Somiedo
name_original: Lagos de Saliencia — circular en el Parque Natural de Somiedo
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo traverse a rugged, mountainous terrain within a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and Natural Park in Asturias, Spain. The region features elevations ranging from 400 to 2,200 meters, offering both challenging climbs and scenic routes. Its landscape is defined by five principal river valleys, lush valleys, dense beech forests, and impressive glacial lakes like the Saliencia Lakes. This environment provides a dynamic setting for touring cyclists seeking routes away from traffic. Best no traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Lagos de Saliencia — circular in the Parque Natural de Somiedo, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the natural park, highlighting the glacial Saliencia Lakes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Curuxéu Thatched Cottage – Lake of the Valley loop from Somiedo, a difficult 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This loop provides views of traditional "teito" huts and passes by the large Lake of the Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Views of the Güérgola Gorge – Alto de la Farrapona loop from Veigas, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with gorge views, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling in Somiedo is defined by rugged mountains, lush valleys, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for challenging climbs and scenic rides across varied terrain. The routes in Somiedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Somiedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available in Somiedo?
A: There are currently 13 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo featured on komoot. These routes are specifically curated to offer a serene cycling experience away from vehicular traffic, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the natural beauty of the region.

Q: What is the typical terrain and difficulty level for no-traffic touring cycling in Somiedo?
A: Somiedo's terrain is rugged and mountainous, characterized by significant elevation changes. All 13 no-traffic touring cycling routes listed are classified as 'difficult' due to substantial climbs and descents. Cyclists should expect a challenging but rewarding experience, often on quiet, sometimes rough, roads or paths through valleys and forests.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo?
A: Yes, several no-traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1044899362/lagos-de-saliencia-circular-in-the-parque-natural-de-somiedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos de Saliencia — circular in the Parque Natural de Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 44 km with significant elevation gain, or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31516545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Curuxéu Thatched Cottage – Lake of the Valley loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along these routes?
A: Somiedo's no-traffic routes offer stunning natural and cultural sights. You can expect to encounter impressive glacial lakes like the Saliencia Lakes (Lago de la Cueva, Lago de la Calabazosa, and Lago del Valle), dense beech forests, and traditional 'teito' huts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Güérgola Gorge – Alto de la Farrapona loop from Veigas&lt;/a&gt; offers dramatic landscapes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31517870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lago de la Cueva – Lake of the Valley loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive lake views.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife while cycling in Somiedo?
A: Somiedo Natural Park is renowned for its rich wildlife. While cycling, you have a chance to spot various species, including deer and capercaillie. The park is also a key habitat for the Cantabrian brown bear, especially in spring, though sightings are rare and often require guided experiences. Always maintain a respectful distance from any wildlife you encounter.

Q: What is the best time of year for no-traffic touring cycling in Somiedo?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Somiedo is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more favorable, and the mountain passes are typically clear of snow. Spring offers lush greenery and potential wildlife sightings, while autumn brings vibrant fall colors to the beech forests. Be prepared for varied mountain weather, even in summer.

Q: Are there any long-distance no-traffic touring cycling options in Somiedo?
A: While many routes are day trips, some can be combined or extended for longer touring experiences. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31517870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lago de la Cueva – Lake of the Valley loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 65 km route. The mountainous terrain and quiet roads lend themselves well to multi-day bikepacking, though specific long-distance, fully car-free routes are less common than interconnected quiet roads.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in Somiedo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the unparalleled tranquility, the dramatic mountain scenery, and the sense of immersion in untouched nature that these no-traffic routes provide. The challenging climbs are frequently mentioned as rewarding, leading to breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo?
A: Given the rugged and mountainous terrain, all 13 no-traffic touring cycling routes in Somiedo are currently rated as 'difficult' on komoot, featuring significant elevation gains. This means they are generally more suitable for experienced cyclists. Families with younger children or those seeking very gentle rides might find these routes too challenging.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for these routes, such as Pola de Somiedo or Veigas. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended parking locations, as facilities can vary. Always park responsibly and respect local regulations.

Q: Are permits required for cycling on these routes in Somiedo Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for cycling on established roads and trails within Somiedo Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to check the official park regulations before your visit, especially if you plan to venture off marked paths or into more sensitive areas. Respecting the park's rules helps preserve its natural beauty.
tips: It's a mountain pass that marks the border between Asturias and León. It's the starting point for countless hikes, including the Saliencia Lakes hike, in a beautiful setting. A magical place, no matter the season.

One of the lakes composing 'Lagos de Saliencia' - It is part of a Natural Park and overnight stays are not allowed. There are guards driving around and using binoculars.

It's a lovely route though and very worth cycling by! Tough loose gravel through, so suspension and thick tires are recommended.
This is an impressive glacial lake located in the Somiedo Natural Park. The lake is situated at an altitude of 1,770 meters and is surrounded by beautiful mountain peaks and alpine forests.
It is not the most beautiful, but it is the final touch of the route from La Farrapona.
